text.title: Design Document Template: Rules and Fill Order
text.summary: Rules for correctly filling the design.md template: section fill order, AD format requirements, FR coverage reconciliation, and anti-patterns that produce incomplete designs.

rule.statement: Every design.md must contain all nine required sections: Reference Documents, Overview, Architecture Decisions, Components, Data Flow, Files, Testing Strategy, Risks, and FR Coverage.
rule.rationale: Missing sections cause executor agents to make ungrounded implementation decisions. The FR Coverage table in particular must be generated last as a completeness check.

list.item: Reference Documents — links to spec.md, constitution.md, contracts.md
Overview — 2-3 sentences of technical approach (write LAST, after all sections are complete)
Architecture Decisions — AD-X in ADR format (minimum 3, maximum 7 for typical features)
Components — component name, purpose, location, dependencies
Data Flow — Component A to B to C with validation, transformation, persistence steps
Files — CREATE/MODIFY/DELETE table with Action, File, Description, FR, AD columns
Testing Strategy — unit tests table and integration tests table
Risks — Impact, Probability, Mitigation for each risk
FR Coverage — every FR-X from spec mapped to AD-X and file entries

rule.statement: Each AD must include Context, Options (minimum 2 alternatives), Decision, Rationale, and Traces to FR-X. Omitting any field makes the AD unusable for future agents making related decisions.
rule.rationale: Future agents reading a design.md need to understand why a path was NOT taken, not just what was chosen. Consequences and rejection reasons are the most valuable fields.

antipattern.description: AD with straw-man alternatives — obviously inferior options listed to justify the pre-decided choice.
antipattern.reason: Agents reviewing the design cannot trust the alternatives analysis and will re-open closed decisions during implementation.

rule.statement: Write Overview last, after all other sections are complete. Fill FR Coverage as the final reconciliation step.
rule.rationale: Overview written first becomes a summary of assumptions. Overview written last summarizes actual decisions. FR Coverage generated last catches any FR that was not assigned to an AD or file during drafting.

list.item: 1. Reference Documents
2. Architecture Decisions
3. Components
4. Data Flow
5. Data Models
6. API Endpoints (reference contracts.md — do not redefine)
7. Files table
8. Testing Strategy
9. Risks
10. FR Coverage (reconciliation)
11. Overview (summary of actual decisions)

rule.statement: If contracts.md exists, the API Endpoints section must say "See contracts.md section X" and must not redefine endpoints.
rule.rationale: Redefining endpoints in design.md creates two sources of truth that drift apart. Executor agents implement from design.md and produce code that diverges from the actual contract.

antipattern.reason: Overview becomes a summary of intentions, not decisions. Write it last.

antipattern.description: Data Models section omits constraints column.
antipattern.reason: Missing constraints cause migration failures when the executor agent implements the schema.
Agents stop after source files. Tests must be explicit CREATE entries or they will not be implemented.

text.reference: Complete design.md template with all required sections
Verifies required sections present and counts ADs and FR traces
